sections.title: Codebase context
sections.content: This is a Phaser 3 + TypeScript game (no React, no backend DB — persistence is `window.localStorage` via three service singletons). I'm mapping your five categories onto their real analogues here: repeated `localStorage` read/parse round-trips (≈ N+1), per-frame redundant work in `Scene.update()` (≈ unnecessary re-renders), in-memory caching over storage parsing, DOM/listener cleanup, and dead/redundant computation in the game loop.

sections.title: 1. Repeated storage reads (N+1-equivalent)
2. Per-frame redundant computation (the "re-render" equivalent)
3. Caching opportunities
sections.content:
- Cache `AuthStorageService`/`ProgressStorageService` reads as above — biggest win for the pattern used across every scene transition.
- `ProgressStorageService.saveProgress()` (`ProgressStorageService.ts:94`) re-serializes the *entire* progress object (all 10 `levelStars`, `introSeen`, flags) on every single-field change like `markIntroSeen`. Fine at this data size, but if progress grows (e.g. per-level metadata), batch or diff writes rather than full-object `JSON.stringify` each time.
- `Preloader.ts` eager-loads both `lv1` and `lv2` gameplay asset sets (`GAMEPLAY_LV1` + `GAMEPLAY_LV2`, ~50 images) before the player even reaches Main Menu, regardless of which level they'll actually play. Given the project's "Level 3+ is placeholder" scope and only 2 playable levels, this is a minor, optional win — not worth restructuring around a single generic `Gameplay` scene per your architecture rules, just flagging it exists.

sections.title: 4. Memory leaks
sections.content:
I did not find real leaks. The DOM/listener cleanup is actually done correctly:
- `AuthForm` (`ui/AuthForm.ts:67`) explicitly registers `scene.events.once('shutdown', () => this.destroy())` and destroys all `GameObjects.DOMElement`s — the one place a leak would be easy to introduce (raw DOM inputs), and it's handled.
- `Hud`, `TouchControls`, `DialogBox`, `ChoicePanel`, `PauseOverlay` all attach listeners directly to Phaser `GameObjects.Image`/`Text`, which Phaser destroys automatically on scene shutdown — no manual cleanup needed there, and none is missing.
- `Gameplay.destroyWorld()` (`Gameplay.ts:188`) correctly removes the marker tween (`markerTween?.remove()`) before nulling it, avoiding a dangling tween reference across `buildSegment()` calls between segments.

Nothing to fix here — worth confirming rather than inventing an issue.

sections.title: 5. Redundant computations
sections.content:
- Covered above: the per-frame `filter().sort()` in `nextInteraction()` and the dead ternary are the concrete instances in this codebase.
- `AuthStorageService.findByUsername()` (`AuthStorageService.ts:110`) does a full `.toLowerCase()` scan of all accounts per call; trivial at current scale (few local accounts), not worth optimizing now.

**Priority if you want me to apply any of these**: #2 (per-frame cache + dead ternary in `Gameplay.ts`) is the only one with a measurable runtime cost today, since it runs 60×/sec during gameplay. The storage-caching items (#1/#3) are architecturally correct but low-impact at current data volume — happy to implement either on your say-so, but per project rules I won't change code without you confirming which of these you want.
